description: Schema cleanup — review three migration scripts dropping tables, renaming columns, and adding constraints across users (3.2M rows) and products (20M rows) tables with 4 dependent services
review_checklist: Check for NOT NULL columns added without DEFAULT values on large tables
Verify indexes are created with CONCURRENTLY on large tables
Check if dropped or renamed columns are still referenced by application code
Check for lock-heavy operations on high-traffic tables
Consider the deployment strategy (rolling/blue-green) and downtime budget

ground_truth.description: Dropping sessions table will fail due to audit_log.session_id FK reference. If forced, it orphans up to 150M audit_log records. audit_service and auth_service still actively read/write to sessions.
Renaming old_price to legacy_price breaks pricing_service and catalog_service which query products.old_price directly. At 50,000 reads/sec this causes widespread failures.
Adding NOT NULL column sku without a DEFAULT on a 20M-row products table causes a full table rewrite with ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock, blocking 50,000 reads/sec and all writes.
Creating index without CONCURRENTLY on a 20M-row products table acquires a SHARE lock, blocking all writes for the duration of the index build.
Adding NOT NULL column preferences (JSONB) without a DEFAULT on a 3.2M-row users table causes a full table rewrite. Use DEFAULT '{}' for a metadata-only operation in PostgreSQL 11+.
Changing email from VARCHAR(255) to TEXT triggers a full table rewrite with ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock on 3.2M rows, blocking all reads and writes to the users table.
